evals.prompt: Equipment carrying amount 90M, tax written-down value 65M, tax rate 30%. What's the deferred tax and which side?
evals.expected_output: Taxable temporary difference 25M (carrying exceeds tax base on an asset) -> deferred tax LIABILITY of 7.5M.
assertions: Correctly identifies it as taxable, producing a DTL not a DTA

evals.prompt: We have 50M of unused tax losses after two consecutive loss years. Our new 5-year forecast shows 30M of taxable profits. Can we book the full 15M DTA at 30%?
evals.expected_output: No. Recent losses require convincing evidence; recognize only to the extent supported - 30M x 30% = 9M DTA, with 20M of losses unrecognized and disclosed. Forecast quality must be assessed.
assertions: Notes the heightened evidence standard after a history of recent losses
Requires disclosure of the unrecognized portion

evals.prompt: The government enacts a rate change from 30% to 25% effective next year. Our DTL is 60M, of which 12M arose through OCI on a building revaluation. Book the remeasurement.
evals.expected_output: Remeasure all deferred balances: new DTL = 60/0.30*0.25 = 50M, release 10M. The OCI-origin portion (12M -> 10M, release 2M) goes through OCI; the remaining 8M release goes through P&L (backward tracing under IAS 12).
assertions: Splits the 10M release between OCI (2M) and P&L (8M) by origin

evals.prompt: Tax rate 30%. For each temporary difference in the attached file (evals/files/temporary_differences.csv), classify it as taxable or deductible, compute the DTL/DTA, and note that the warranty provision is a LIABILITY (carrying 250,000, tax base nil) and the revaluation difference goes through OCI.
evals.expected_output: PP&E: taxable TD 300,000 -> DTL $90,000. Warranty: deductible TD 250,000 -> DTA $75,000. Revaluation: taxable TD 200,000 -> DTL $60,000 recognized in OCI.
assertions: Notes the revaluation deferred tax is recognized in OCI, not P&L
